outputPrompt: Create a five-image independent vertical set inspired by a botanical floral-form lighting object. Generate five separate images sequentially from Image 1 through Image 5. Do not create a collage, grid, long image, multi-panel canvas, or contact sheet. Each image must be an independent 3:4 vertical smartphone still-life photograph. All five images must show the exact same lamp design and the selected lighting type. The botanical form, shade structure, glowing core, support curve, base or mounting logic, material palette, switch position, cable behavior when relevant, and handmade signature detail must remain consistent across all five images. Before generation, internally choose one uncommon Botanical Design Family for this run and keep it consistent across all five images. Do not default to lily, lotus, orchid, rose, tulip, generic white flower lamps, or blue vase-base lamps. Translate the botanical inspiration into the lamp's shade, diffuser, support, joint, base, or mounting language; never attach real flowers to the lamp. Image 1: Single Lamp Cover. Show the completed same botanical lamp as a beautiful home still-life object, lamp on, warm glow emerging from the floral-form diffuser, grey-white wall, dark tabletop or dark grounding surface, realistic contact shadow, no text. Image 2: Daylight Off. Natural daytime side light, same lamp switched off, full object view, readable overall proportion, shade form, support, base or mounting, dark grounding point, no text. Image 3: Daylight On. Natural daytime side light, same angle or similar angle to Image 2, same lamp switched on, warm core visible inside the selected flower-form shade, no heavy night mood, no text. Image 4: Flower Form Detail. Smartphone close-up of the same lamp's most recognizable botanical detail: petal edge, bell shade, star diffuser, folded petal, pod core, cluster diffuser, line botanical support, brass vein, joint, base texture, and material thickness. Image 5: Quiet Home Scene. Place the same lamp in a real quiet home corner such as bedside table, window-side table, black side cabinet, console, or reading corner. Warm lamp glow should land on tabletop, wall, paper, cloth, or nearby object. Keep it real, calm, and not hotel-rendered.
consistencyPrompt: All five images must depict the exact same lighting product, adapted to the selected lighting type. Do not change the lamp design, botanical family, flower-form silhouette, diffuser material, glowing core count, support curve, base or mounting structure, material palette, color accents, switch position, or cable logic between images. Each new generation run should choose a different uncommon botanical family from previous runs when possible.
layoutPrompt: Five independent 3:4 vertical images. Never combine them into one canvas. No 2×2 grid. No nine-grid. No collage. No long poster. No split-screen. No captions. No text. No labels. No logo. No watermark. Every image must look like a natural smartphone still-life photograph rather than a commercial catalog render.
avoidPrompt: Pendant lamp when another lighting type was selected
Floor lamp when another lighting type was selected
Wall lamp when another lighting type was selected
Ceiling lamp when another lighting type was selected
Real flowers attached to the lamp
